working_on: ADR 0056 routine-protection refinement: convert no-token+no-diary hard-fail to mode-aware soft-warn for routine mode; uniform LOUD bodies across modes for mempalace-availability surfaces; pending-diary index for deferred recovery.
declared_scope: Close the remaining hard-fail gap in ADR 0056's mempalace-availability contract. Engine sessions retain hard-fail; routine sessions get a new soft-warn `mempalace_diary_write_skipped_routine` and an entry in a new `engine/session/diary_pending_index.json`. Drop the engine/routine LOUD-vs-standard body differentiation for substrate-availability soft-warns — apply LOUD body uniformly because archive review is the routine-side visibility surface. Document the deferred-diary recovery procedure. phase: NA-engine-apparatus
outcome_summary: Closed the residual hard-fail S-0090 left standing in ADR 0056's mempalace-availability contract, plus collapsed the engine/routine LOUD-body differentiation, plus added a deferred-diary recovery affordance for routine sessions. Three changes: (1) New soft-warn `mempalace_diary_write_skipped_routine` (routine mode only) replaces the engine-mode-only `mempalace_diary_write_skipped` HARD-FAIL for routines. Engine retains hard-fail (asymmetry justified by unattended-vs-interactive difference: engine has immediate fix path; routine cannot be killed by intermittent MCP per user directive). Side effect: routine path appends to a new `engine/session/diary_pending_index.json`. (2) LOUD body uniformly across modes for the four substrate-availability soft-warns (substrate_at_close, acknowledged_skip, substrate_intermittent, skipped_routine). Drops S-0089/S-0090 engine/routine differentiation per user pushback ('I have to know that happened clearly in the session text'). Archive review is the routine-side visibility surface; the LOUD prefix costs nothing extra in routine archives. (3) Pending-diary index `engine/session/diary_pending_index.json` (mutable, committed). Boot-time SessionStart hook surfaces count + IDs in LOUD attention block when non-empty. Recovery procedure documented at `engine/operations/routine-mode-operations.md` 'Deferred diary recovery': user reconnects MCP, opens interactive session, reads each pending archive, authors diary entry from structured fields, calls mempalace_diary_write, removes entry. ADR 0056 amended in place with third Decision-history entry (no supersession). 8 new pytests in TestRoutineProtectionS0091 + 5 existing TestSubstrateTokenTightening tests updated for LOUD-uniform body assertions. 37 mempalace tests + 855 engine/tools tests pass; mypy --strict clean. Validator at shutdown: 0 hard-fails; soft-warns hold at baseline (366 missing_rigor_score Phase-5 carryover, 1 health_check_overdue per the campaign-deferral, 1 pre-existing adr_consequences_deliverable_audit, 10 issue_collision expected for in-scope Issues). MemPalace MCP confirmed live (substrate probe OK; 5 tool calls captured). Third campaign-plan shift in same campaign (Issue #41 (C3) S-0091 → S-0092); user has named this tradeoff explicitly. Decision drawer captured in `paideia/decisions` room.
